title: Default bullish crossover strategies pass CrossUp operands backwards

evidence.quote: NewCrossUpIndicatorRule stores the first argument as upper, the second as lower, and checks lower > upper.
The test calls NewCrossUpIndicatorRule(dnInd, upInd) and expects the rising upInd to cross above dnInd.

reasoning: The primitive CrossUp rule expects the indicator that should cross upward to be the second argument. The registry passes fast/signal/close as the first argument, so default bullish entries are inverted and can trigger on bearish relationships instead of the advertised bullish crosses.
reproduction: Instantiate ema_cross_fast or macd_cross and evaluate it across a known bullish fast-above-slow or MACD-above-signal crossover; the entry rule is wired to the opposite operand relationship.
recommendation: Swap only the CrossUp operands in the affected strategy factories: slowMA/fastMA, slowEMA/fastEMA, signal/macd, and bbLower/closeInd. Keep CrossDown calls as-is where they already model the downward crossing operand order.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: strategy_registry_test.go only checks listing, instantiation, serialization, and unstable periods. cross_test.go verifies the primitive rule, but no registry test asserts ShouldEnter/ShouldExit behavior on a crossover series.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add table tests for each default crossover strategy using fixed indicators or a crafted series where the fast/MACD/price line crosses upward, asserting ShouldEnter is true only for the bullish crossing.
minimumFixScope: pkg/trading/strategy_registry.go operand order plus behavioral registry tests.
